[BugFix] Cap DSv4 DSA admission to startup pool-sizing bound (#9548)

### What this PR does / why we need it?
Mirrors the admission-cap mechanism added in vLLM PR
[#40946](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/40946) into the DSv4
/ DSA path of vllm-ascend. Fixes the long-prompt hang reproduced on
Ascend, equivalent to the upstream cliff reported in
vllm-project/vllm#40863 (observed on H200 for DSv4-Pro).

**Fix**:
- Thread `max_num_batched_tokens` and `max_model_len` through
`AscendHybridKVCacheCoordinator` into `get_manager_for_kv_cache_spec`.
- For `MLAAttentionSpec` with `compress_ratio>1`, set
`max_admission_blocks_per_request = cdiv(max_model_len //
compress_ratio, block_size) + 1`,
matching the startup pool sizer. Compressed MLA does not shrink via
recycling like SWA, but never exceeds this bound either.
- Default the new arg to `max_model_len` when unset so non-DSA call
sites collapse to the previous uncapped behavior.

**Workaround until this lands**: `--no-scheduler-reserve-full-isl` at
serve time (re-enables the pre-#37307 chunk-based admission, at the cost
of giving up the thrash protection that flag was designed for).

- vLLM version: v0.20.2
- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/1ac10f159a09897baada01b14b6a0dd6442aefd6
---------
Signed-off-by: liuchenbing <chenliumail@163.com>
Signed-off-by: Mengqing Cao <cmq0113@163.com>
Co-authored-by: liuchenbing <chenliumail@163.com>
Co-authored-by: wangzhao-11a <AikenLynchktv@scotlandmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Mengqing Cao <cmq0113@163.com>

@@ -11,7 +11,13 @@
     SingleTypeKVCacheManager,
     spec_manager_map,
 )
-from vllm.v1.kv_cache_interface import FullAttentionSpec, KVCacheSpec, MLAAttentionSpec
+from vllm.v1.kv_cache_interface import (
+    ChunkedLocalAttentionSpec,
+    FullAttentionSpec,
+    KVCacheSpec,
+    MLAAttentionSpec,
+    SlidingWindowSpec,
+)
 from vllm.v1.request import Request
 
 
@@ -204,9 +210,50 @@ def find_longest_cache_hit(
         return computed_blocks
 
 
-def get_manager_for_kv_cache_spec(kv_cache_spec: KVCacheSpec, **kwargs) -> SingleTypeKVCacheManager:
+def get_manager_for_kv_cache_spec(
+    kv_cache_spec: KVCacheSpec,
+    max_num_batched_tokens: int | None = None,
+    max_model_len: int | None = None,
+    **kwargs,
+) -> SingleTypeKVCacheManager:
+    """Build the per-spec KV cache manager.
+
+    For DSv4 / DSA path (``MLAAttentionSpec`` with ``compress_ratio>1``), align
+    the runtime admission gate with the startup pool-sizing bound the same way
+    vLLM PR #40946 does for ``SlidingWindowSpec`` / ``ChunkedLocalAttentionSpec``.
+    Without this cap, an admitted request can demand more blocks than the pool
+    was sized to back, and ``allocate_slots`` silently returns ``None`` from
+    the ``full_sequence_must_fit`` branch, leaving long-input requests stuck
+    in the waiting queue (see vLLM issue #40863, observed on DSv4 + MTP with
+    cc>=1 and prompt>=32K).
+
+    The compressed-MLA peak per request is bounded by
+    ``cdiv(max_model_len // compress_ratio, block_size)`` (it does not shrink
+    via recycling like SWA, but neither does it ever exceed this). Capping at
+    this value matches the pool sizer and makes admission consistent with the
+    block budget actually held.
+    """
     manager_class = spec_manager_map[type(kv_cache_spec)]
     if isinstance(kv_cache_spec, MLAAttentionSpec) and kv_cache_spec.compress_ratio > 1:
         manager_class = CompressAttentionManager
+        if max_model_len is not None:
+            # Compressed-MLA peak in blocks: ceil(max_model_len/compress/block).
+            compress_ratio = kv_cache_spec.compress_ratio
+            block_size = kv_cache_spec.block_size
+            max_compressed_tokens = max_model_len // compress_ratio
+            kwargs["max_admission_blocks_per_request"] = cdiv(max_compressed_tokens, block_size) + 1
+    elif isinstance(kv_cache_spec, (SlidingWindowSpec, ChunkedLocalAttentionSpec)):
+        # Replicate the upstream PR #40946 cap setting for recycling specs.
+        # We override the vLLM factory above, so the upstream block that does
+        # this lives in dead code (never reached); without re-applying it here
+        # SlidingWindowMLASpec / ChunkedLocalAttentionSpec groups have no cap
+        # and ``full_sequence_must_fit`` admission reserves the full
+        # ``max_model_len`` worth of blocks per request, exhausting the pool
+        # at cc>=2 on DSv4 (see vLLM issue #40863).
+        if max_num_batched_tokens is not None and max_model_len is not None:
+            kwargs["max_admission_blocks_per_request"] = kv_cache_spec.max_admission_blocks_per_request(
+                max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
+                max_model_len=max_model_len,
+            )
     manager = manager_class(kv_cache_spec, **kwargs)
     return manager

         eagle_attn_layer_names: list[str] | None = None,
         metrics_collector: KVCacheMetricsCollector | None = None,
+        max_num_batched_tokens: int | None = None,
     ):
         self.kv_cache_config = kv_cache_config
         self.max_model_len = max_model_len
         self.enable_caching = enable_caching
+        # Fall back to `max_model_len` when unset so the recycling-aware
+        # admission cap (vLLM PR #40946) collapses to the prior uncapped
+        # behavior. The scheduler always supplies the real value at runtime.
+        if max_num_batched_tokens is None:
+            max_num_batched_tokens = max_model_len
+        self.max_num_batched_tokens = max_num_batched_tokens

[Xlite][Feature] Xlite support glm4.7 w8a8 quantization (#9415)

### What this PR does / why we need it?
This PR adds support for w8a8 quantization of the GLM-4.7 model to the
xlite module. Specifically, it introduces shared expert weight
processing and refactors the w8a8 weights of xlite, making subsequent
adaptation easier.

- vLLM version: v0.19.1
- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/b1388b1fbf5aaef47937fabe98931211684666a6

Signed-off-by: wangxiaoran <wangxiaoran11@huawei.com>
Co-authored-by: wangxiaoran <wangxiaoran11@huawei.com>

[CI]: Auto-update estimated test times in config.yaml (#9532)

## Summary

This PR was auto-generated by the **Update estimated test times**
[workflow](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/actions/runs/26386617709).

It updates the `estimated_time` values in
`.github/workflows/scripts/config.yaml` based on actual elapsed times
collected from CI workflow runs.

### Methodology

- Each e2e test job uploads its elapsed time as a `timing-data-*`
artifact upon completion.
- The workflow aggregates all collected timing artifacts across jobs.
- For each test, the **median** elapsed time is computed to reduce
outlier impact.
- A **10% safety buffer** is applied and the result is rounded to the
nearest 10 seconds.

[Ops][BugFix] Disable SwiGLU clamp by default (#9438)

### What this PR does / why we need it?

This PR changes SwiGLU clamp/limit defaults from sentinel large values
to 0 across the Python config path, torch binding schemas, op
definitions, and tiling defaults. Kernel clamp vector ops now run only
when the received clamp/limit value is positive, so the default value
disables clamp instead of doing no-op-ish clamp work.

For grouped_matmul_swiglu_quant_weight_nz_tensor_list, the existing
kernel skip guard is preserved and this PR only changes the
default/validation side. Covered paths include dequant_swiglu_quant,
grouped_matmul_swiglu_quant, grouped_matmul_swiglu_quant_v2, and
grouped_matmul_swiglu_quant_weight_nz_tensor_list.

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

No intentional user-facing API change. Explicit positive
swiglu_limit/clamp_limit values keep the previous clamp behavior.
Omitting the value now means clamp is disabled with 0.

### How was this patch tested?

- bash -n csrc/build_aclnn.sh
- git diff HEAD~1..HEAD --check
- git diff upstream/main..HEAD -- csrc/build_aclnn.sh
vllm_ascend/models/deepseek_v4.py

[Attention][Feature] Implement compute-communication overlap for pure-prefill batches (#9504)

What this PR does / why we need it?

This PR implements a delayed allgather optimization for pure-prefill
batches in DeepSeek V4 DSA attention. Each
rank computes wq_a/wkv on its local SP partition first, then allgathers
smaller intermediates instead of the full
hidden_states. Communication and computation are overlapped via
dual-stream (main + aux) scheduling.

  Does this PR introduce any user-facing change?

Yes, adds a new configuration parameter prefill_comm_compute_overlap
(default: disabled).

  How was this patch tested?

Verified correctness (output logits match baseline) on DeepSeek V4 with
TP+SP, pure prefill and PD-disaggregated
  prefill scenarios.

- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/1ac10f159a09897baada01b14b6a0dd6442aefd6

commits.message:
[BugFix] Fixed the issue where cv_indexer_qkv_prepare's multistream parallel capability was not enabled when multistream-dsv4-dsa-overlap=true (#9530)

## What this PR does / why we need it?

When `multistream_dsv4_dsa_overlap=true` and `compress_ratio==4`, the
dual-stream DSA path's Phase 1 (`dsa_decode_prepare` /
`dsa_prefill_prepare`) was using the serial `_indexer_qkv_prepare`
instead of the multistream `cv_indexer_select_qli`. This caused the
Indexer QKV prepare stage to lose V/C/AIV engine parallel scheduling
opportunities.

## Changes

- **Add `_cv_indexer_qkv_prepare_multistream()`** which extracts Part0-3
from `cv_indexer_select_qli` with internal dual-stream parallel
scheduling, excluding Part4 (weights_proj, q_quant, QLI) that remain in
the kvcompressor phase for overlap:
  - Part0: qr_quant + compressor + kv_hadamard (main stream pre-compute)
  - Part1: matmul[C] ∥ kv_quant[V] + scatter_k_cache[AIV]
  - Part2: rope[V] (main stream serial)
  - Part3: q_hadamard[C] ∥ scatter_scale_cache[AIV]
- **Update `dsa_decode_prepare` / `dsa_prefill_prepare`** to call the
new multistream method instead of serial `_indexer_qkv_prepare`
- **Update `dsa_forward` Phase 2** to replace `_indexer_quant_scatter`
with direct `npu_dynamic_quant` on `q_idx` since KV scatter is now done
inside the multistream prepare
- **Fix `DSAPrepareResult` type alias** from 10 to 9 elements

## Execution flow after this PR

```
_mla_prolog_multistream → _cv_indexer_qkv_prepare_multistream → kvcompressor dual-stream overlap
       (Phase 1)                    (Phase 1)                            (Phase 2-3)
```

Each phase has internal dual-stream parallel scheduling on V/C/AIV
engines.

## Does this PR introduce any user-facing change?

No. Only affects the internal scheduling when
`multistream_dsv4_dsa_overlap=true` and `compress_ratio==4`. The serial
path (`multistream_dsv4_dsa_overlap=false`) and `compress_ratio==128`
path are unchanged.

## How was this patch tested?

- Code review: verified mathematical equivalence between serial and
multistream paths
- Verified `DSAPrepareResult` type alias consistency
- Verified no dead code introduced (all existing serial path methods
remain used by non-dual paths)
- NPU hardware testing pending

Co-authored-by: ltdo111 <1061328217@qq.com>
Co-authored-by: songyang <songyang719@163.com>

- vLLM version: v0.20.2
- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/1ac10f159a09897baada01b14b6a0dd6442aefd6

---------

Signed-off-by: MosCloud <bwzhang1991@163.com>
Co-authored-by: ltdo111 <1061328217@qq.com>
Co-authored-by: songyang <songyang719@163.com>
